reviews.author: Rik Riel (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch is no longer needed and decided to drop it due to potential harm caused by watermark boosting, agreeing to remove it from the series.
sentiment_signals: acknowledged a fix is not needed
reviews.raw_body:
I'll just drop this patch for now.

While we no longer need it, and the watermark boosting
can cause a little bit of harm at times, this probably
isn't worth the extra code churn in this series.

-- 
All Rights Reversed.

2026-05-27.patch_summary: This patch series aims to improve the allocation of large memory blocks by dividing memory into superpageblocks and steering unmovable, reclaimable, and high-atomic allocations into those already tainted by such allocations. The goal is to leave as many 1GB superpageblocks available for movable allocations, which can be easily defragmented for regular or huge pages. Various strategies are employed to achieve this, including preferential allocation from tainted blocks, fallback to small pages for certain kernel allocations, and migration of movable memory when a block falls below a free threshold. This approach enables more extensive use of 2MB THPs and mTHPs, as well as reliable allocation of 1GB pages. The series is built on top of Johannes's PCPBuddy series, which aims to reduce the need for CPUs to get pages from zone free lists by relying on page block ownership.
